title: Export Code Generation Enhancements with Quoting and CASE Statement Fixes
story: As a data migration specialist, I want granular control over quoting styles for objects, fields, and values in my SQL output, and correctly formatted CASE statements for picklist mappings, so that the generated code is ready to execute in my target database.
description:
Major enhancements to the SQL code generation export, driven by customer demo feedback:

**Three separate quoting dropdowns:**
1. **Objects and Fields**: Double Quotes, Brackets, Backticks, None — applied to object and field identifiers
2. **Values**: Double Quotes, Single Quotes, No Quotes — applied to static values in CASE statements
3. **Null/Mapping Placeholder**: NULL, '', "" — determines how unmapped or empty fields are rendered

**CASE statement fixes:**
- Remove comma between THEN and WHEN clauses (was: `THEN "Open", WHEN` → should be: `THEN 'Open' WHEN`)
- Each WHEN clause on its own line for readability
- Empty picklist source values represented by the selected null placeholder (default: NULL)
- ELSE NULL clause added before END
- Picklist values wrapped in single quotes (values quoting), not double quotes

**Other fixes:**
- Remove trailing comma after the last field in the SELECT statement
- Default the Null dropdown to NULL
acceptance_criteria: Three separate quoting dropdowns for Objects/Fields, Values, and Null handling
Objects and Fields dropdown offers Double Quotes, Brackets, Backticks, None
Values dropdown offers Double Quotes, Single Quotes, No Quotes
Null dropdown offers NULL, empty single quotes, empty double quotes — defaults to NULL
CASE WHEN statements have no comma between THEN and WHEN
Each WHEN clause appears on its own line
Empty picklist values use the selected null placeholder
ELSE NULL clause included before END
No trailing comma after the last field in SELECT
Picklist mapped values use the Values quoting style
ux_changes: Export modal redesigned with three dropdown controls for Objects/Fields quoting, Values quoting, and Null handling. Layout updated for clarity.
middleware_changes: SQL generation engine updated with three independent quoting modes. CASE statement formatting fixed: no comma between THEN/WHEN, each WHEN on new line, ELSE NULL added, proper value quoting. Trailing comma removal logic for last field.
implementation_notes: The three-dropdown approach was refined through multiple iterations. The CASE statement formatting required several passes to get right — initially values were being double-quoted instead of single-quoted, and empty mapping values were not being handled. A future enhancement for static value quoting was identified and deferred.
comment_summary: Developer needed clarification on the purpose of Values and Null dropdowns. Multiple iterations were needed: first pass fixed comma and line breaks, second pass addressed value quoting (single vs double), third pass handled empty picklist values with the null placeholder. A reference SQL example was provided to illustrate the expected output.
